User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FarmFriend application.

CropForSale.title: Crop For Sale
CropForSale.description: Represents a crop listed for sale by a user.

userId.description: The ID of the user who listed the crop.

cropName.description: The name of the crop being sold.

quantity.description: The quantity of the crop available for sale (in kg).

price.description: The price per unit (e.g., per kg).

status.description: The current status of the listing.

listedAt.description: The timestamp when the crop was listed.

CropDiagnosis.title: CropDiagnosis
CropDiagnosis.description: Represents a crop diagnosis performed by the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the crop diagnosis.

userId.description: Reference to the User who requested the diagnosis. (Relationship: User 1:N CropDiagnosis)

imageUri.description: URI of the uploaded image for diagnosis.

confidenceScore.description: The confidence score of the diagnosis (0-100).

organicRemedies.description: Description of the organic remedies for the diagnosed issue.

chemicalTreatments.description: Description of the chemical treatments for the diagnosed issue.

preventionTips.description: Description of the prevention tips for the diagnosed issue.

diagnosisDate.description: The date and time of the diagnosis.

WeatherForecast.title: WeatherForecast
WeatherForecast.description: Represents weather forecast data for a specific location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the weather forecast entry.

location.description: The location for which the forecast is provided (e.g., city name, coordinates).

forecastDate.description: The date and time for which the forecast is valid.

condition.description: The weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy, Cloudy).

severeWeatherAlert.description: Description of any severe weather alerts for the location.

MarketPrice.title: MarketPrice
MarketPrice.description: Represents the market price of a specific crop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the market price entry.

cropName.description: The name of the crop (e.g., Tomato, Wheat).

marketName.description: The name of the market where the price is recorded.

price.description: The price of the crop per unit (e.g., kg, ton).

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the price (e.g., kg, ton).

priceTrend.description: The price trend (e.g., up, down, stable).

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the price was recorded.

id.description: Unique identifier for the soil condition entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose soil condition is being recorded. (Relationship: User 1:N SoilCondition)

phLevel.description: The pH level of the soil.

nitrogenLevel.description: The nitrogen level in the soil.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the soil condition was recorded.

ForumPost.title: ForumPost
ForumPost.description: Represents a post in the knowledge hub community forum.

id.description: Unique identifier for the forum post.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the post. (Relationship: User 1:N ForumPost)

question.description: The question posted by the user.

postDate.description: The date and time when the post was created.

ForumReply.title: ForumReply
ForumReply.description: Represents a reply to a forum post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the forum reply.

postId.description: Reference to the ForumPost this reply belongs to. (Relationship: ForumPost 1:N ForumReply)

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the reply. (Relationship: User 1:N ForumReply)

replyDate.description: The date and time when the reply was created.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all crops listed for sale.

params.description: The unique identifier for the crop listing.

params.name: soilConditionId

definition.description: Subcollection for storing forum posts created by the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: forumPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the forum post.

definition.description: Subcollection for storing forum replies to a specific post.

params.name: forumPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the forum post.

params.name: forumReplyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the forum reply.

definition.description: Collection for storing weather forecast data.

params.name: weatherForecastId
params.description: The unique identifier for the weather forecast entry.

definition.description: Collection for storing market price data.

params.name: marketPriceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the market price entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability for the FarmFriend application. User-owned data (CropDiagnoses, SoilConditions, ForumPosts, and ForumReplies) is structured under the `/users/{userId}` path to simplify ownership-based security rules. This approach leverages hierarchical paths for user-owned data, enabling secure and efficient data management. A new top-level collection `cropsForSale` is added to manage all crop listings centrally, which simplifies querying for all available crops across different users.

Authorization Independence is achieved through this hierarchical structure, avoiding the need for `get()` calls in security rules.  Each subcollection's documents inherit implicit authorization based on their location within the `/users/{userId}` path.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) are supported through structural segregation. Private user data is stored under the `/users/{userId}` collection, while global data like `WeatherForecast`, `MarketPrice`, and `cropsForSale` are stored in separate collections, each with its own security rules. This prevents mixing data with different access needs within the same collection.

The structure employs consistent naming conventions for fields like `userId` and `id` to ensure predictability and maintainability. The explicit modeling of user-owned data and global data further enhances clarity and simplifies security rule definitions.
